name: John Cornelius
title: Senior Program & Operations Leader
summary: Program and operations leader with 20+ years delivering multimillion-dollar initiatives across defense, manufacturing, and logistics. Known for turning complex, multi-stakeholder programs into steady, on-time execution while building teams that stay. Fluent in Lean Six Sigma, ERP and analytics tooling, and the quiet work of safety, readiness, and cost discipline. Comfortable in the field, the boardroom, and the data.

Data, Systems & Continuous Improvement: Power BI, SQL, and Python for analytics
Safety, Compliance & Leadership: OSHA and PSM programs; audit-ready operations
Safety culture building across shifts and sites

experience.title: Senior Operations & Analytics Manager
duties: Lead modernization and operations programs across multi-site production, maintenance, logistics, and controls.
Own scope, schedule, and budgets for capital and continuous-improvement initiatives; manage risk registers and vendor performance with structured cadences.
Build SCADA- and BI-driven dashboards for throughput, downtime, quality, and safety; translate the numbers into decisions the line can act on.
Coach plant leaders and frontline teams; partner with HR, EHS, and finance to turn priorities into day-to-day behavior.
achievements: Raised throughput by 12% while cutting overtime cost 22% through paired Lean and TPM programs.
Sustained 600+ days lost-time injury free with zero PSM findings across external audits.
Improved DIFOT by 15% and held inventory accuracy above 99.5% through S&OP and logistics rework.
Negotiated supplier and freight terms to deliver $2.3M+ in validated savings while increasing delivery reliability.

experience.title: Senior Program Manager — Aviation & Advanced Systems
duties: Directed $48M+ aviation and asset-management programs across training, operations, and maintenance organizations.
Stood up predictive analytics and dashboards to raise readiness and sharpen maintenance planning.
Wrote and standardized evaluation criteria, SOPs, and safety protocols adopted across multiple units.
Advised senior leaders and coached mid-level managers and operators through complex, high-stakes decisions.
achievements: Raised qualification pass rates to 97% through standardized curricula and performance management.
Maintained zero Class A or Class B safety incidents across a decade of high-tempo operations.
Delivered complex, multi-stakeholder programs on schedule and under budget in expeditionary environments.
Built and retained high-performing teams across duty stations, cultures, and operational tempos.

experience.title: Aviation Officer & Instructor Pilot
duties: Led small-unit operations, aircrew training, and aviation safety programs.
Planned and executed missions under time-constrained conditions with high accountability for people and equipment.
Built the operational foundation for later program-management work: risk, logistics, safety, and team leadership.
achievements: Recognized for operational performance and instructor ratings across multiple assignments.
Trained and mentored dozens of junior leaders and operators who went on to senior roles.

education.degree: Master of Science — Unmanned Systems (Human Factors)
education.details: Human–machine integration, decision-making, and safety for unmanned systems.
Technical foundation in systems, safety, and logistics for aviation program oversight.

certifications.name: CS50's Artificial Intelligence with Python (in progress)

affiliations: Civil Air Patrol — sUAS training and community programs
Veteran; community volunteer leadership in Eastern Panhandle, WV

body: I'd like to bring twenty years of program, operations, and data leadership to your team. I've run multimillion-dollar portfolios in defense and industry, and I build the kind of quiet, dependable operations leaders trust.
I pair classical program discipline with real data fluency — Lean Six Sigma, SAP/ERP, Power BI, SQL, Python — and I do the hands-on coaching that makes a program actually stick on the floor.
